cross_scene_arcs.title: Bond with Ellie and the Dream of Paradise Falls
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carl's relationship with Ellie evolves from playful beginnings to shared sacrifice and enduring legacy, centered on their dream of Paradise Falls, which ultimately becomes a catalyst for his emotional renewal.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carl meets Ellie and joins her imaginative world, beginning their shared mission to retrieve the balloon.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carl discovers Ellie's message encouraging him to start a new adventure, shifting his focus from fulfilling the past to embracing the future.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: How to honor Ellie's memory by beginning a new chapter remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Defense of Home and Escalation with Developer
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carl resists external pressure to leave his home, escalating from verbal defiance to physical confrontation when symbols of his life with Ellie are threatened.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carl publicly refuses to sell his house and mocks the developer.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carl attacks a worker who damages Ellie's mailbox, crossing into physical violence to protect a personal relic.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Legal and social consequences of his actions remain pending, and developer threats continue.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reluctant Caregiver and Emotional Reengagement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carl transitions from isolation to connection by reluctantly accepting Russell, forming a protective bond, and ultimately risking himself to save the boy and their shared makeshift family.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carl reluctantly allows Russell into his house, breaking his self-imposed solitude.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carl physically anchors the house to save Russell, Dug, and Kevin, fully embracing a protective role.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Carl still questions his capacity to fulfill this new responsibility, though he acts decisively in crisis.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confrontation with Muntz and Shattered Illusions
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carl's admiration for Muntz turns to conflict as he uncovers the explorer's true nature, leading to a physical struggle where Carl defends Kevin and resists Muntz's obsession.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carl expresses reverence for Muntz, linking him to Ellie's dream.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carl fights Muntz directly, using improvisation to resist despite being overpowered, and works to protect the bird and children.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Muntz remains a threat, and the immediate danger of the confrontation is unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Journey of the House and Survival in Descent
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carl navigates the perilous descent of his house, from recognizing the danger of impact to physically preventing it from falling off the dirigible, marking a shift from passive traveler to active protector.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carl realizes the house is descending too fast and dangerously close to the ground.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carl secures the house to the dirigible by holding the hose, preventing it from falling despite ongoing threats.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The house remains precariously positioned, and escape from the dirigible is not yet achieved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Quest for Redemption Through Proof
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Muntz's singular mission to capture the bird evolves from a vow to clear his name into an obsessive, increasingly violent pursuit that defines his identity and actions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Publicly disgraced and expelled, vowing to prove the beast's existence
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Armed and physically attacking Carl to reclaim the bird, willing to kill
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His need for external validation remains unfulfilled at the time of his fall

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Isolation and Paranoia
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Muntz transitions from a celebrated explorer to a self-imprisoned recluse whose authority is maintained through control and fear, culminating in his physical and symbolic fall.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Stripped of status and exiled from the Explorer's Society
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Presumed dead after falling from the dirigible, isolated in defeat

cross_scene_arcs.title: Performance of Heroism vs. Reality of Obsession
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Muntz initially presents himself as a charismatic, heroic figure but gradually reveals the depth of his obsession, exposing the facade of his explorer persona.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Reintroduces himself with charm and control, masking his true motives
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: His obsession laid bare as he destroys his own trophies and fixates on the caged bird
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The authenticity of his past adventures remains ambiguous

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalation of Conflict with Carl and Russell
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Muntz's relationship with Carl and Russell shifts from hospitality to suspicion, then open hostility, culminating in violent confrontation and pursuit.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Welcomes them as guests, establishing friendly rapport
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Armed and attacking them directly, attempting to kill or capture
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The conflict ends with Muntz's fall, but no reconciliation or resolution of his grievances

cross_scene_arcs.title: Pursuit of the Assisting the Elderly Badge
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Russell persistently seeks to earn the Assisting the Elderly badge by offering help to Carl, is manipulated into a false task, gradually becomes a genuine helper, and ultimately earns the badge when Carl awards him the Ellie Badge.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Russell initiates contact with Carl to fulfill badge requirements but is rebuffed.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Russell is awarded a special badge by Carl, achieving his goal through authentic connection rather than checklist completion.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Evolution of Russell and Carl's Relationship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Russell transitions from an unwelcome intruder to a trusted companion as Carl gradually shifts from resistance to emotional openness, culminating in a father-son bond.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carl repeatedly rejects Russell's offers of help and manipulates him into leaving.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carl publicly recognizes Russell as a son-like figure and embraces him emotionally.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Russell's Emotional Vulnerability and Need for Belonging
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Russell's motivation to earn the badge is rooted in his desire for his absent father's approval; this emotional need is gradually met through Carl's growing presence and validation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Russell expresses hope that his dad will attend the Senior Explorer ceremony.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carl steps in as a father figure when no parent appears, fulfilling Russell's need for recognition and belonging.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Russell's biological father remains emotionally unavailable.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Russell's Role in Protecting Kevin
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Russell becomes increasingly committed to protecting Kevin, challenging Carl's decisions, acting independently, and ultimately risking danger to ensure Kevin's safety and reunion with her young.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Russell names Kevin and physically shields her from Carl's rejection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Russell defies Carl to pursue Kevin mid-air and later helps defend her from external threats.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Kevin's safety remains at risk due to ongoing conflict with Muntz.
